steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists examples from three fields. Each list item must begin with a location marker (in astronomy, in chemistry, in mathematics) to maintain parallel structure. A colon should introduce the list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘abound: in astronomy,’: Colon correctly introduces the list; in astronomy starts the first item, matching in chemistry and in mathematics that start the others. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘abound in astronomy:’: The first list item then lacks a location marker, breaking parallelism with the other two items. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘abound, in astronomy,’: Incorrect punctuation for introducing a list. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘abound in astronomy;’: Incorrect punctuation for separating list items. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
